policy.title: RBI finalizes climate scenario analysis requirements for scheduled commercial banks
policy.description: RBI issued final guidelines requiring all scheduled commercial banks with assets over ₹50,000 crore to conduct climate scenario analysis by March 2027. Banks must integrate physical and transition risk into ICAAP submissions starting FY2027-28.

policy.title: SEBI expands BRSR Core to top 500 listed companies with assurance mandate
policy.description: SEBI mandated BRSR Core reporting with reasonable assurance for top 500 companies by market cap effective FY2026-27, expanding from prior top 150. Value chain disclosure scope extended to cover 75% of upstream suppliers by value.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI releases exposure draft on parametric insurance product framework
policy.description: IRDAI published draft guidelines establishing regulatory sandbox parameters for parametric climate insurance, permitting index-based triggers for flood, drought, and cyclone perils. Comments due by 15 May 2026.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee issues final Pillar 3 climate risk disclosure framework
policy.description: BCBS finalized climate-related Pillar 3 disclosure requirements effective January 2028 for G-SIBs, with phased rollout to all internationally active banks by 2030. Includes standardized metrics for financed emissions and physical risk exposures.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk granularity
policy.description: NGFS published updated scenarios incorporating IPCC AR6 WG3 pathways with regional physical risk overlays at sub-national level. New chronic risk variables for water stress and agricultural yield added for South Asia.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

policy.title: ISSB confirms interoperability guidance for emerging market jurisdictions
policy.description: IFRS Foundation issued guidance enabling proportionate ISSB S2 adoption for emerging markets, allowing phased Scope 3 reporting and simplified scenario analysis for smaller reporters through 2029.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence partners with HDFC Bank for physical risk integration
competitors.description: Jupiter announced a strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to integrate ClimateScore Financial into the bank's credit risk systems for corporate and MSME lending. Implementation targets coverage of ₹2 lakh crore in loan exposures by Q3 2026.

competitors.title: Sprih raises $12M Series A to expand climate risk analytics for Indian NBFCs
competitors.description: Bangalore-based Sprih closed a $12M Series A led by Nexus Venture Partners to build physical risk scoring for NBFC portfolios. Company claims 15 NBFC clients with combined AUM of ₹80,000 crore.

competitors.title: Moody's launches India-specific physical risk scores at district level
competitors.description: Moody's ESG Solutions released Climate Risk Scores for India with district-level granularity covering flood, cyclone, heat stress, and water scarcity. Product designed for RBI climate disclosure compliance.

competitors.title: XDI signs data partnership with CRISIL for India asset-level risk analysis
competitors.description: Australian climate analytics firm XDI partnered with CRISIL to combine physical risk models with Indian credit data. Joint product targets banks seeking integrated PD adjustment for climate hazards.

competitors.title: Watershed launches financed emissions module for emerging market banks
competitors.description: Watershed released a dedicated financed emissions tracking product for banks in emerging markets, with pre-built emission factors for 12 Indian sectors. Pricing targets mid-market financial institutions.

competitors.title: Enture selected by SBI for BRSR value chain data collection platform
competitors.description: Enture won a contract with State Bank of India to deploy its ESG data collection platform across SBI's corporate banking clients for BRSR value chain disclosures. Contract valued at approximately ₹8 crore over 3 years.

research.title: Climate Risk and Bank Lending in Emerging Markets: Evidence from South Asia
research.description: Study of 450 banks across South Asia finds 1-standard-deviation increase in flood exposure correlates with 23 basis point increase in NPAs within 18 months. Recommends explicit climate risk weights in provisioning frameworks.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance for Climate Resilience in India
research.description: IFC analysis projects India's parametric insurance market could reach $2.5 billion by 2030 with enabling regulation. Identifies MSME lending and agricultural value chains as highest-potential segments for bank-distributed products.

research.title: Sigma 2/2026: Natural Catastrophe Losses and Insured Gaps in Asia
research.description: Swiss Re reports 2025 Asian natural catastrophe economic losses at $78 billion with only 12% insured, lowest protection gap globally. India accounted for $18 billion in losses with 8% insurance penetration.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Capital Flows
research.description: CPI research finds 68% of listed companies in India lack Scope 3 emissions data versus 31% in EU, constraining foreign ESG fund allocations by an estimated $14 billion annually. Calls for proportionate disclosure pathways.

research.title: Supervisory Expectations for Climate Risk Management: Emerging Market Implementation
research.description: BIS survey of 28 emerging market central banks finds only 6 have issued binding climate risk guidelines for banks. Paper provides implementation roadmap with 18-month minimum for scenario analysis capacity building.

research.title: Integrating Physical Climate Risk into Credit Ratings: Methodology and Evidence
research.description: CRISIL proposes framework adjusting issuer ratings by up to 2 notches for high physical risk exposure. Back-testing on 200 Indian corporates shows 14% would face downgrade pressure under RCP 4.5 by 2035.

ourRead.headline: RBI's final climate scenario mandate just made physical risk quantification a compliance necessity for Indian banks—StepChange must move from pilot conversations to production contracts within 90 days.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: climate risk has crossed from voluntary ESG initiative to hard regulatory requirement across Indian banking. The RBI's finalized climate scenario analysis guidelines (policy-1), combined with Basel's Pillar 3 disclosure framework (policy-4) and SEBI's expanded BRSR assurance mandate (policy-2), create a compliance cliff that every large Indian bank must now prepare for. The research evidence is catching up too—the World Bank's finding of a 23 bps NPA impact from flood exposure (research-1) gives banks concrete evidence they need PD/LGD adjustments, not just disclosure checkboxes.

The RBI mandate is the most consequential policy signal. Banks with assets over ₹50,000 crore must now build climate scenario analysis capabilities by March 2027—that's 23 months away. The requirement to integrate physical and transition risk into ICAAP submissions starting FY2027-28 creates a direct procurement trigger for StepChange's core product. Every conversation we've been having with risk teams just got budget approval cover. However, the NGFS Phase V scenarios (policy-5) adding sub-national South Asia granularity means our models must match or exceed this resolution to remain credible against global benchmarks.

The competitor landscape is intensifying faster than expected. Jupiter's HDFC Bank partnership (comp-1) and XDI's CRISIL alliance (comp-4) represent well-capitalized global players locking up marquee Indian relationships. Moody's district-level India product (comp-3) directly targets RBI compliance. Meanwhile, Sprih's $12M raise (comp-2) shows domestic competitors are now funded to compete. Our edge remains our India-native data and deeper integration with local credit systems, but the window to establish category leadership is narrowing. The SBI-Enture deal (comp-6) also signals that large PSU banks are actively procuring—we should be in those conversations.

The IFC's parametric insurance sizing (research-2) projecting a $2.5 billion market by 2030 validates our second strategic bet, especially as IRDAI's draft framework (policy-3) opens the regulatory sandbox. The Swiss Re protection gap data (research-3) showing India's 8% insurance penetration against $18 billion in losses is the commercial case for bank-distributed parametric products. We should use the IRDAI comment period to position StepChange as technical partner for product design. The CPI data gaps research (research-4) directly supports our global south data thesis—the $14 billion allocation constraint from missing Scope 3 data is a commercial opportunity if we can fill it credibly.

Watch: Jupiter-HDFC implementation progress over the next quarter will set expectations for what 'good' looks like in this market. Action: The team should prepare three RFP-ready packages at different price points targeting the top 20 banks by assets before the RBI mandate creates a vendor selection scramble in Q3.
